title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in evaluation and control: new work probes whether models’ behavioral tendencies can be measured more directly, while benchmark design and interaction-aware testing continue to mature. On the deployment side, fresh papers focus on inference-time safety steering, privacy-preserving learning, and translating regulatory text into machine-readable rules that could support more auditable AI systems.

items.title: Google outlines evaluations for behavioral dispositions in LLMs
items.why_it_matters: It pushes alignment assessment beyond task accuracy toward measuring stable model behavioral tendencies.

items.title: Google examines how many raters AI benchmarks really need
items.why_it_matters: Benchmark reliability affects whether safety and capability claims are statistically trustworthy.

items.title: Modular Energy Steering proposes inference-time control for safer text-to-image models
items.why_it_matters: Inference-time safeguards could improve image-model safety without retraining or major quality tradeoffs.

items.title: De Jure uses iterative self-refinement to extract regulatory rules from legal text
items.why_it_matters: Structured rule extraction could make compliance requirements more auditable and machine-actionable.

items.title: Bayesian vertical federated learning targets multimodal survival prediction with privacy
items.why_it_matters: It addresses privacy-preserving training for sensitive distributed data while exposing predictive uncertainty.

items.title: User-turn generation is proposed as a probe of interaction awareness in language models
items.why_it_matters: It tests whether models anticipate downstream conversational effects, a gap in standard evaluations.

items.title: Memory forgetting techniques aim to reduce false memory propagation in autonomous agents
items.why_it_matters: Agent memory management is a practical safety issue for long-horizon reliability and error containment.

items.title: Google discusses responsible disclosure of quantum risks to cryptocurrency security
items.why_it_matters: It highlights coordinated disclosure and risk communication practices for emerging security threats.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
